title: Store Music and Retail Media: The Channel Nobody Runs — Daniel Fox
meta_description: Retail media is a gold rush to sell the screen by the door. The most behaviorally active channel in the store, the music, plays to every shopper all day and is owned by no one. Why in-store audio is the blind spot of the retail-media boom.

schema.headline: Store Music and Retail Media: The Channel Nobody Runs
schema.description: Retail media is racing to sell in-store screens while the music, the channel that reaches every shopper all day and actually moves behavior, goes unmanaged. A music behaviorist on the in-store audio blind spot.
